name: cave-bear-lair
summary: A natural cave den occupied by a large predator. Bones, matted bedding of leaves and fur, deep claw-marks in the walls. No structure — the cave IS the room.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: irregular — deeper than wide, one fat chamber with a narrow mouth

accent.purpose: nest depression — trampled bedding at the back

primary_palette.note: back-of-cave nest with the kill-pile piled against the wall
matted bedding of leaves, moss, and shed fur — centerpiece of the lair
past victim half-eaten, gnawed clean — grisly centerpiece
recent kill being slowly consumed — flies, spatter

story_prompts: occupied — the bear is sleeping at the back, scent thick in the air
recently vacated — still warm, bear out hunting, returns in d6 hours
the bear is dying of a festering wound — dangerous but slow
not a bear — an owlbear, or something worse wearing a bear's den
a cub nest — mother away, three cubs, moral dilemma baked in

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: cave-dark — near-zero ambient, daylight bleeds in from the mouth only

anti_patterns: no built walls — cave outline is stone, use rounded/irregular cell shapes
no furniture, no tools — this is an animal's home, not a humanoid's
do not arrange bones neatly — scatter with heavy concentration at the nest
keep it dark — this is a place adventurers bring their own light into
